stories.headline: Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire leads to Groq raising $650M in internal funding
synopsis: Groq is raising $650 million in internal funding after Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire.
This investment will help Groq pivot from hardware to focus on AI software development.
The success of this funding round will be crucial for Groq's future growth and competitiveness in the AI market.
stories.explanation: Imagine a company changing its focus from building cars to creating the software that powers them, that's what Groq is doing with this investment.

stories.headline: Nvidia launches Cosmos 3 for physical AI reasoning and action models
synopsis: Nvidia has launched Cosmos 3, a new platform for developing physical AI reasoning and action models.
This platform will enable developers to create more realistic and interactive AI models.
The success of Cosmos 3 will depend on its adoption by developers and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of Cosmos 3 like a simulator that helps AI models learn how to interact with the physical world, like a virtual playground for AI.

stories.headline: Expanse launches to unlock wasted GPU capacity
synopsis: Expanse has launched a new platform to help unlock wasted GPU capacity in cloud computing.
This platform will enable companies to make better use of their GPU resources and reduce waste.
The success of Expanse will depend on its ability to deliver on its promises and attract a large customer base.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a super powerful computer that's only using a fraction of its power, Expanse helps to unlock that unused power.

stories.headline: UniScale achieves adaptive unified inference scaling via online joint optimization
synopsis: Researchers have developed UniScale, a new method for adaptive unified inference scaling.
UniScale uses online joint optimization to improve the efficiency of large language models.
The success of UniScale will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of UniScale like a smart manager that helps AI models work more efficiently by adjusting their settings on the fly.

stories.headline: GLIDE library enables reliable evaluation of agentic systems
synopsis: Researchers have developed the GLIDE library for reliable evaluation of agentic systems.
GLIDE provides a framework for evaluating the performance of AI models in complex environments.
The success of GLIDE will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a set of tools that helps you evaluate how well an AI model is performing in a complex situation, that's what GLIDE provides.

stories.headline: TRINE achieves token-aware, runtime-adaptive FPGA inference engine for multimodal AI
synopsis: Researchers have developed TRINE, a token-aware, runtime-adaptive FPGA inference engine for multimodal AI.
TRINE enables more efficient and flexible processing of multimodal data.
The success of TRINE will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of TRINE like a special kind of computer chip that's designed to handle multiple types of data at the same time, like a Swiss Army knife for AI.

stories.headline: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode
synopsis: Researchers have identified a gap in physical AI inference capabilities, specifically in batch-1 LLM decode.
This gap is due to memory-bound limitations rather than bandwidth limitations.
The success of addressing this gap will depend on the development of new technologies and architectures.
stories.explanation: Imagine trying to pour water into a bucket that's too small, that's what's happening with memory-bound limitations in AI inference.

stories.headline: Correcting Split Selection in Online Decision Trees via Anytime-Valid Inference
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for correcting split selection in online decision trees.
This method uses anytime-valid inference to improve the accuracy of decision trees.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this method like a tool that helps decision trees make more accurate decisions by correcting mistakes in real-time.

stories.headline: GPU Forecasters: Language Models as Selective Surrogates for Kernel Runtime Optimization
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for using language models as selective surrogates for kernel runtime optimization.
This method enables more efficient optimization of GPU kernels.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a smart assistant that helps optimize the performance of computer programs, that's what this method provides.

stories.headline: MedCoG: Maximizing LLM Inference Density in Medical Reasoning via Meta-Cognitive Regulation
synopsis: Researchers have developed MedCoG, a method for maximizing LLM inference density in medical reasoning.
MedCoG uses meta-cognitive regulation to improve the performance of LLMs in medical applications.
The success of MedCoG will depend on its adoption by the medical community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of MedCoG like a tool that helps AI models think more critically and make better decisions in medical applications.

stories.headline: From Weak Cues to Real Identities: Evaluating Inference-Driven De-Anonymization in LLM Agents
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for evaluating inference-driven de-anonymization in LLM agents.
This method enables the assessment of LLM agents' ability to de-anonymize individuals.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models can identify individuals from limited information, that's what this method provides.

stories.headline: How does Bayesian Sampling help Membership Inference Attacks?
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the role of Bayesian sampling in membership inference attacks.
Bayesian sampling enables more efficient and effective membership inference attacks.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of Bayesian sampling like a special kind of statistical tool that helps AI models make more accurate predictions, even when the data is limited.

stories.headline: Human-Alignment and Calibration of Inference-Time Uncertainty in Large Language Models
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for human-alignment and calibration of inference-time uncertainty in LLMs.
This method enables more accurate and reliable uncertainty estimates in LLMs.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps AI models understand how certain they are about their predictions, that's what this method provides.

stories.headline: OBCache: Optimal Brain KV Cache Pruning for Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
synopsis: Researchers have developed OBCache, a method for optimal brain KV cache pruning.
OBCache enables more efficient and effective LLM inference.
The success of OBCache will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of OBCache like a special kind of computer chip that's designed to handle long-context LLM inference more efficiently.

stories.headline: Empirical Characterization of Inference-Time Elicited Probability Transformations in Large Language Models
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the empirical characterization of inference-time elicited probability transformations in LLMs.
This research enables a better understanding of how LLMs make predictions.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps understand how AI models make predictions, that's what this research provides.

stories.headline: PhyDrawGen: Physically Grounded Diagram Generation from Natural Language
synopsis: Researchers have developed PhyDrawGen, a method for physically grounded diagram generation from natural language.
PhyDrawGen enables the generation of diagrams that are grounded in physical reality.
The success of PhyDrawGen will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of PhyDrawGen like a tool that helps AI models create diagrams that are based on real-world physics.

stories.headline: Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit: Disentangling Evolution Capabilities in Self-Evolving LLM Agents
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the relationship between harness updating and harness benefit in self-evolving LLM agents.
This research enables a better understanding of how LLM agents evolve and improve.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps understand how AI models learn and improve over time, that's what this research provides.

stories.headline: EHRBench: An Automated and Reliable EHR-based Benchmark for Clinical Decision Making with LLMs
synopsis: Researchers have developed EHRBench, a benchmark for clinical decision making with LLMs.
EHRBench enables the evaluation of LLMs in clinical decision making tasks.
The success of EHRBench will depend on its adoption by the medical community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of EHRBench like a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models can make decisions in medical applications.

stories.headline: MAVEN: Improving Generalization in Agentic Tool Calling
synopsis: Researchers have developed MAVEN, a method for improving generalization in agentic tool calling.
MAVEN enables more effective and efficient tool calling in LLM agents.
The success of MAVEN will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps AI models learn how to use tools more effectively, that's what MAVEN provides.

stories.headline: Learning Agent-Compatible Context Management for Long-Horizon Tasks
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for learning agent-compatible context management.
This method enables more effective and efficient context management in LLM agents.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this method like a tool that helps AI models understand and manage context in long-horizon tasks.

synopsis: Researchers have developed PReMISE, a method for policy rubrics as measurement specifications.
PReMISE enables more effective and efficient evaluation of LLM judges.
The success of PReMISE will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models make decisions based on policies, that's what PReMISE provides.

stories.headline: Planner-Centric Reinforcement Learning for Deep Research with Structure-Aware Reward
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for planner-centric reinforcement learning.
This method enables more effective and efficient deep research with structure-aware reward.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this method like a tool that helps AI models learn how to plan and make decisions in complex research tasks.

stories.headline: COMPASS: Cognitive MCTS-Guided Process Alignment for Safe Search Agents
synopsis: Researchers have developed COMPASS, a method for cognitive MCTS-guided process alignment.
COMPASS enables more effective and efficient search in safe search agents.
The success of COMPASS will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps AI models search for solutions in a safe and efficient manner, that's what COMPASS provides.

stories.headline: Distilling LLM Feedback for Lean Theorem Proving
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for distilling LLM feedback for lean theorem proving.
This method enables more effective and efficient theorem proving in LLMs.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this method like a tool that helps AI models learn how to prove theorems more efficiently.

stories.headline: BilliardPhys-Bench: Benchmarking Physical Reasoning and Visual Dynamics of Multimodal LLMs
synopsis: Researchers have developed BilliardPhys-Bench, a benchmark for physical reasoning and visual dynamics.

The success of BilliardPhys-Bench will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models understand physical phenomena, that's what BilliardPhys-Bench provides.

stories.headline: A Persona-Based Evaluation Framework for Pluralistic Alignment in Generative AI
synopsis: Researchers have developed a persona-based evaluation framework for pluralistic alignment.
This framework enables the evaluation of generative AI models in terms of their alignment with human values.
The success of this framework will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this framework like a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models align with human values and perspectives.

synopsis: Researchers have developed HADT, a heterogeneous multi-agent differential transformer.

The success of HADT will depend on its adoption by the space community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps control a group of satellites working together to observe the Earth, that's what HADT provides.

stories.headline: GraphARC: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Graph-Based Abstract Reasoning
synopsis: Researchers have developed GraphARC, a benchmark for graph-based abstract reasoning.

The success of GraphARC will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of GraphARC like a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models can reason about complex relationships and graphs.

stories.headline: LLM-FACETS: A Privacy-Preserving Framework for Evaluating LLM Transparency and Accountability
synopsis: Researchers have developed LLM-FACETS, a framework for evaluating LLM transparency and accountability.
LLM-FACETS enables the evaluation of LLMs in terms of their transparency and accountability.
The success of LLM-FACETS will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps evaluate how transparent and accountable AI models are, that's what LLM-FACETS provides.

stories.headline: COLLEAGUE.SKILL: Automated AI Skill Generation via Expert Knowledge Distillation
synopsis: Researchers have developed COLLEAGUE.SKILL, a method for automated AI skill generation.
COLLEAGUE.SKILL enables the generation of AI skills via expert knowledge distillation.
The success of COLLEAGUE.SKILL will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of COLLEAGUE.SKILL like a tool that helps AI models learn new skills by distilling knowledge from experts.

stories.headline: Learning to Adapt: Self-Improving Web Agent via Cognitive-Aware Exploration
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for learning to adapt in self-improving web agents.
This method enables more effective and efficient exploration in web agents.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps AI models learn how to adapt and improve themselves in complex web environments.

stories.headline: AutoSci: A Memory-Centric Agentic System for the Full Scientific Research Lifecycle
synopsis: Researchers have developed AutoSci, a memory-centric agentic system for scientific research.
AutoSci enables the automation of the full scientific research lifecycle.
The success of AutoSci will depend on its adoption by the scientific community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of AutoSci like a tool that helps automate the entire process of scientific research, from hypothesis to publication.

stories.headline: LinTree: Improving LLM Reasoning with Explicitly Structured Search Histories
synopsis: Researchers have developed LinTree, a method for improving LLM reasoning.
LinTree enables more effective and efficient reasoning in LLMs via explicitly structured search histories.
The success of LinTree will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps AI models reason more effectively by keeping track of their search history.

stories.headline: When LLM Reward Design Fails: Diagnostic-Driven Refinement for Sparse Structured RL
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the failure of LLM reward design in sparse structured RL.
This research enables a better understanding of how to refine LLM reward design.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this research like a tool that helps understand how to fix problems with AI models' reward systems.

stories.headline: Gradient-Free Training of Spiking Neural Networks via Low-Rank Evolution Strategies
synopsis: Researchers have developed a method for gradient-free training of spiking neural networks.
This method enables more efficient and effective training of spiking neural networks.
The success of this method will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps train AI models that mimic the brain's neural networks more efficiently.

stories.headline: Hamiltonian-Inspired Attention Mechanism for Scalable RF Transmitter Fingerprinting
synopsis: Researchers have developed a Hamiltonian-inspired attention mechanism for RF transmitter fingerprinting.
This mechanism enables more efficient and effective RF transmitter fingerprinting.
The success of this mechanism will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this mechanism like a tool that helps identify and track radio frequency transmitters more efficiently.

stories.headline: When LLMs Learn to Be Consistently Wrong: A Multi-Model Study of Linear Representations of Synthetic Deception
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the phenomenon of LLMs learning to be consistently wrong.
This research enables a better understanding of how LLMs can be deceived and how to prevent it.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps understand how AI models can be tricked into making wrong predictions and how to prevent it.

stories.headline: LLMs Without Deep Neural Networks: New Architecture, Benefits and Case Study
synopsis: Researchers have developed a new architecture for LLMs that does not rely on deep neural networks.
This architecture enables more efficient and effective LLMs.
The success of this architecture will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of this architecture like a new design for AI models that doesn't use the traditional deep neural network approach.

stories.headline: Social Reasoning in Machines: Investigating Collective Truth-Seeking Dynamics in Large Language Model Debate
synopsis: Researchers have investigated the phenomenon of social reasoning in machines.
This research enables a better understanding of how AI models can engage in collective truth-seeking dynamics.
The success of this research will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps understand how AI models can work together to find the truth in debates and discussions.

stories.headline: NumLeak: Public Numeric Benchmarks as Latent Labels in Foundation Models
synopsis: Researchers have developed NumLeak, a method for using public numeric benchmarks as latent labels.
NumLeak enables more efficient and effective training of foundation models.
The success of NumLeak will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Think of NumLeak like a tool that helps AI models learn from publicly available data and improve their performance.

stories.headline: CodeGolf Bench: A Multi-Language Benchmark for Evaluating Concise Code Generation Capabilities of Large Language Models
synopsis: Researchers have developed CodeGolf Bench, a benchmark for evaluating concise code generation capabilities.
CodeGolf Bench enables the evaluation of LLMs in terms of their ability to generate concise code.
The success of CodeGolf Bench will depend on its adoption by the research community and its ability to deliver on its promises.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that helps evaluate how well AI models can generate short and efficient code, that's what CodeGolf Bench provides.

stories.headline: Groq raises $650M in internal funding after Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire
synopsis: Groq has raised $650M in internal funding after Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire.
This investment will help Groq pivot from hardware to focus on AI software development.
The success of this funding round will depend on Groq's ability to deliver on its promises and compete in the AI market.
stories.explanation: Imagine a company changing its focus from building cars to creating the software that powers them, that's what Groq is doing with this investment.
